boundary_c_constraints: ACTIVE_CONTEXT_STATE.json remains the only live-state authority.
ACTIVE_CONTEXT_SCHEMA.json, ARIS_BOOT.md, DECISION_LOCKS.md, ROADMAP_CANONICAL.md, scripts/validate_active_context_state.py, and artifacts/ remain active-contract inputs.
CURRENT_STATE.md, NEXT_ACTION.md, CONTEXT_INDEX.md, ARIS_PHASE_LEDGER.md, and archive/derived_mirrors/* must not remain active-contract inputs.
Root untracked mirror files remain ignored, unstaged, and noncanonical.
archive/derived_mirrors/* remain historical or derived only and must not become live-state authority.

current_incompatibilities.incompatibility: current_state_mirror, next_action_mirror, context_index_mirror, and phase_ledger_history still encode active root markdown mirror paths.
markdown_mirror_validation_required=true conflicts with the selected no-active-markdown-mirrors boundary.
The helper validates archive/derived_mirrors markdown files, which is incompatible with a future active contract that excludes markdown mirrors.
test_mirror_sync_passes keeps assert_mirror_sync.py in the active local validation suite.
ROADMAP_CANONICAL and historical artifacts still preserve assert_mirror_sync as a historical deliverable, which must remain historical rather than active.

recommended_actions: Remove artifact_routes entries that advertise CURRENT_STATE.md, NEXT_ACTION.md, CONTEXT_INDEX.md, and ARIS_PHASE_LEDGER.md as active contract paths.
Remove those mirror keys from the artifact_routes required list or reclassify them into an explicitly historical-only object if preserving audit metadata is necessary.
Set validation_policy.markdown_mirror_validation_required to false or replace it with wording that no longer implies active markdown mirror gating.
Preserve state_file, schema_file, validator_script, readme, roadmap_authority, and boot/ledger references that remain active under BOUNDARY_C.
must_not_do: Do not re-point active mirror fields to archive/derived_mirrors/* as a substitute active contract.
Do not require ACTIVE_CONTEXT_STATE.json mutations for this normalization.

rationale: The helper is not part of the GitHub workflow and therefore can be retired from the active contract without workflow churn.
Keeping it as an active or CI-required check would contradict BOUNDARY_C by preserving markdown mirrors as validation inputs.
The file may remain on disk for historical audit, but future default validations must stop treating it as an active requirement.
specific_future_actions: Remove assert_mirror_sync.py from the future active validation matrix and from default local validation expectations.
Optionally add a file header or ledger note in the future repair clarifying that the helper is historical-only and not a routing gate.
Do not delete the helper in the first normalization repair unless a separate cleanup phase is explicitly authorized.

rationale: The current validator already anchors on ACTIVE_CONTEXT_STATE.json and current PURG route invariants.
Observed active contradictions are in schema path declarations and helper/test usage, not in the validator's current live-route logic.
validator_normalization_plan.guardrail: If implementation of the future schema/helper retirement reveals a hidden validator dependency on mirror fields, block the repair and emit a new active-context-only blocker artifact instead of widening scope.

recommended_actions: Retire or rewrite tests/test_validate_active_context.py::test_mirror_sync_passes so local default validation no longer requires assert_mirror_sync.py.
Add or adjust focused tests to assert that the active contract no longer requires root markdown mirrors or archive/derived_mirrors paths.
Preserve tests/test_purg04_validator_scope_repair.py unchanged unless a hidden validator dependency is discovered.
must_not_do: Do not add tests that recreate root markdown mirror authority.
Do not add tests that promote archive/derived_mirrors/* to active routing inputs.

recommended_actions: Record the normalization repair result in DECISION_LOCKS.md, explicitly stating that markdown mirrors are no longer active-contract inputs.
Update README.md only if needed to avoid future operator confusion about mirror expectations; keep any change minimal and aligned with BOUNDARY_C.
Do not touch archive/superseded/* or archive/derived_mirrors/* in the first normalization repair.

root_untracked_file_policy.future_repair_rule: They remain outside the future patch unless an explicit later cleanup phase authorizes deletion or archival handling.

archive_derived_mirror_policy.future_repair_rule: Leave archive/derived_mirrors/* untouched in the first normalization repair; only de-activate references to them from active schema/helper/test surfaces.

excludent_policy_observed.observed_rule: excludent/ is not active context, not source-of-truth, and not prompt input except for explicit forensic review.

rejected_plans.rejection_reason: Insufficient because assert_mirror_sync.py and test_mirror_sync_passes would still keep markdown mirrors in the effective active validation path.
Wider than necessary for the first repair; formal historical-mode behavior adds extra patch surface when retirement from the active contract already resolves the contradiction.
Evidence is sufficient to define a smallest safe future patch without operator tie-break.

future_patch_scope: Normalize ACTIVE_CONTEXT_SCHEMA.json away from active markdown mirror declarations.
Retire scripts/assert_mirror_sync.py from the active contract without deleting historical evidence.
Update local tests that currently require assert_mirror_sync.py as an active default check.
Record the normalization result in DECISION_LOCKS.md and, only if needed, minimally clarify README.md.

notes: Current GitHub workflow runs scripts/validate_active_context_state.py and scripts/assert_no_unauthorized_fixtures.py only.
The future normalization repair should preserve workflow success without requiring workflow file edits.

rollback_strategy_for_future_patch: Stage only active-context files in the approved future patch scope.
If schema/helper/test normalization causes validator or local test regression, revert only the future normalization patch before push and emit a blocker artifact instead of widening scope.
Do not rollback or touch pre-existing out-of-scope local files.
blocker_conditions: Any need to mutate ACTIVE_CONTEXT_STATE.json to implement BOUNDARY_C.
Any need to promote archive/derived_mirrors/* into active validation inputs.
Any requirement to recreate or stage root markdown mirror files.
Any hidden dependency that forces scripts/validate_active_context_state.py changes in the same repair.
Any need to touch Project_ARIS, archive/superseded/*, or excludent/ contents.
Any CI regression that cannot be fixed within the approved future file scope.
diagnostics_run: git grep -n 'current_state_mirror|next_action_mirror|phase_ledger_history|context_index_mirror|archive/derived_mirrors|CURRENT_STATE.md|NEXT_ACTION.md|CONTEXT_INDEX.md|ARIS_PHASE_LEDGER.md|assert_mirror_sync' -- ACTIVE_CONTEXT_SCHEMA.json scripts tests README.md DECISION_LOCKS.md ARIS_BOOT.md ROADMAP_CANONICAL.md || true

artifact_sha256.self_hash_limitation: The terminal file sha256 is reported externally after write because a file cannot embed its own final sha256 without self-reference drift.
